subject: Gaming
title: Kickstarter: Crowdfunding Dreams
description: A short lesson to help you apply Kickstarter: Crowdfunding Dreams.

hook.text: Fans funded games before they existed. Kickstarter let players decide what got made—not publishers.

buildup.text: Double Fine's Broken Age raised $3.3 million in 2012. It shattered Kickstarter records and proved the model.

discovery.text: Shovel Knight, Pillars of Eternity, and Undertale all used crowdfunding to bypass traditional publishing.

twist.text: But many funded projects failed to deliver. Mighty No. 9 became infamous for disappointing its backers.

climax.text: Kickstarter democratized funding but shifted risk to players. Backing a game is a gamble, not a purchase.

punchline.text: Players became publishers. Some dreams shipped. Some didn't.

quiz.question: What risk does crowdfunding shift to players?
options: Players bear the risk since backed projects might not deliver
There is no risk to players
Players must help develop the game
